title: crepis × turicensis
intro: Crepis × turicensis, also known as Turicum Hawk's-beard or Crepis turicensis, is a rare hybrid species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional European herbal medicine, where it is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Crepis × turicensis are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Crepis × turicensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal remedy.
